facts.src: Forest Health Grants | CAL FIRE ... CAL FIRE’s Forest Health Program funds active restoration
funds active restoration and reforestation activities aimed at providing for more resilient and sustained forests
Forest Health Grant Solicitation for Concept Proposals is now closed.
Applicants will be notified of their proposal status by May 18, 2026.
please contact Mike Sintetos at mike.sintetos@fire.ca.gov
Non-profit 501(c)(3) or 501(c)(6) organizations (e.g., fire safe councils, land trusts)
Forest Health projects must further the regulatory goals of the Global Warming Solutions Act of 2006
The total project area should aim to be no less than 800 acres in size
should be no less than $750,000, with a maximum allowable request of $7 million
complete all needed environmental compliance work within one year of award and must demonstrate progress toward completing the work within six months of award
The Forest Health Program is part of California Climate Investments
Some activities, such as planning, research, or education, may not have a measurable GHG emissions benefit. In this case, applicants must provide a justifiable qualitative description
